title: {Username} translating to "a form submission" instead of the user who submitted the form?
excerpt: I just want to know which user submitted the form in my email notification.

posts.author: Scott-Pigeon (Scott Sosebee)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just want to know which user submitted the form in my email notification.

Screenshot 2024-09-29 at 11.21.48 AM1920×1121 91.7 KB

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Scott,
You can use the Creation Log column to identify the user who submitted the form (assuming the form submitter is a Monday member)

posts.author: Scott-Pigeon (Scott Sosebee)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks so much! However, when I use the Creation Log, it only shows the date, not the submitter. (Granted I’m testing this and I am the submitter, but that shouldn’t matter?)

posts.author: jonbowser (Jonathan Bowser)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same issue with my forms. I’m currently using a workaround that uses an automation to assign the item creator (i.e. the person submitting the form) to a People column. I then reference that People column in my email. It’s a hack at best, and you have to hope that automations run in the right order for that to get set before your email automation is triggered.
